Hey guys and welcome to the workbook for module 3. And what I really want to do here is I just want to quickly recap some of the main takeaways from the previous lesson to just make sure they're fresh and present before we get into the work. And then we're going to explore the questions, the direct response 2.0 styled questions pulled from the 47 questions workbook that are really going to help fuel the messaging and extract the content needed to work on the theme plates. And then we're going to take a look at the theme plates themselves in separate lessons right underneath this one to go deeper into each one and giving you the tools and the encouragement to execute them. So with that in mind segmentation. So segmentation can happen automatically, you know, if someone opts in for a lead magnet or signs up to your list via Facebook ad or partner traffic, but it can also come in the form of a call out email that gets your prospect on that special launch list. And the big point here is that even if you plan to market your offer to your entire list, it's still a good idea to send out that call out email, which would give your prospect the chance to just feel excited about the outcome. But more importantly, here's the big one, just feel exclusively empowered to achieve it and commit towards achieving it in the most friction free part of your funnel. This is where we open up that ever powerful consistency loop, you know, where we get to say, I thought you wanted this, you told me you wanted this. And now that there's an offer your backtracking, you know, so that's really what you want to hold them accountable to not in a manipulative sleazy way, but in a way that like you really wanted this here is your chance. And you know, it's not that there's a product or an or an investment in front of you. There's an opportunity to do it. So that's what we're aiming for. That's why you want to make sure you are sending out that call out email wherever possible. And then in the confirmation sequence, we learned that this is the most under leveraged email in most marketing funnels. Not only is this usually the most open email in the sequence, but it's your first real chance to diagnose and reflect back intent while adding extra layers of meaning to the previous action. You know, this is where it's no longer just a benign action. Like you've got your newsletter or your lead magnet or video sequence like woohoo, right? This is where you could actually reflect back motivation and diagnosis, which builds that trust builds that credibility and that magical sense of wow, this person really gets me. And that's what you're going for, because that's what will set the stage for the conversion later on. And while this can arguably be accomplished as a video on the landing page or the confirmation page, I highly recommend using it as an email, you know, primarily for some form of this. It's just kind of an easier way to grasp it in my opinion, but there's no harm in doing both. So confirmation email, super under leveraged. This is your chance to really do it the right way and use it as a tool for the final conversion, not just has a benign thing confirming a benign action. So what we're coaching, what these two sub sequences combined are coaching itself, selection, its excitement around the outcome, it's exclusively empowered to achieve it based on those achievements, circumstances or other preconditions that put them at a unique advantage to gain the outcome and allow them to cash in on those privilege points because that feels good. There's that loss aversion and not cashing in on privilege points. So give your prospect that chance and the opportunity to really get that buy-in in the most friction-free environment. You know, there's no offer here. There's no harm in just saying, yeah, I want that because there's no offer to get in the way of it or to really create that friction. So open that consistency loop that will close in the sales sequence. Then we're selling the meaning behind the click. So the intent, the motivation, what they'll no longer stand for or accept, what they're ready to do. You know, what are they taking a stand for by making that click? So add meaning to it. Don't make it that benign technical action of just clicking a mouse and opting in. Give it some more power and juice behind it. And then I just wanted to explore a few different sources of exclusive empowerment. We already did this before. We have this over here. So we could be looking at previous distinctions and accomplishment. So author of a best-selling book speaks basic Italian, already has a six-figure business, and so forth. So that could be one source. It could also be situational, circumstantial, or geographical. You know, they could be a father of a newborn child working remotely, recently laid off. All these things could work. And it could also be things they've endured. Because remember, this is what we feel a lot of pride around. We really take a lot of pride in our battle scars. So let those battle scars be worth something. Let those battle scars be the entry ticket into a new, awesome, amazing opportunity. So here's the big part. These are the direct response 2.0 questions to explore. And it's possible you've already seen them. If you did the 47 questions all in one shot, but if not, here they are again. So what skills, characteristics, experiences, circumstances, or achievements, or points of pride exclusively empower your prospect to achieve the end result and or embark on the journey? So is it that their website already has 2,000 visitors per month? Is it that they already speak basic French? Is it that they only just began losing their hair? Remember that source of exclusive empowerment is write it down here. And what missteps, regrets, sunk costs, or other sources of disillusionment might they need to forgive themselves or be asked for forgiveness for, which you'll see in the Sory template below the Bieber template before being able to get excited about the outcome again. So this is extremely powerful. We just need somebody to say, sorry, on behalf of all those things that have just steered us wrong before we feel excited and courageous enough to re-embark. And how can any of those previous failures be reframed as necessary prerequisites for where they are now? So this ties in with that exclusive empowerment, but it gives them a chance to make that pain worth something, to cash in on that, to cash in on a previous failure. Cashing in on a previous failure to embark on a new opportunity makes that previous failure kind of feel good now. It's been kind of forgiven on a smaller level, not as big as the one you're going to explore later in the closing sequence with your product now involved. But how can those previous failures, what struggles can they bring to the table and cash in as an entry ticket to this journey? And here are just some additional questions. These aren't in the 47 questions book, but these are ones I want you to explore right now. In what ways can you add meaning to the click? So what does it say about them, their commitment level, what they really want, what they'll no longer stand for? And then this one we did explore in module two, what is that moment of highest tension? So I want you to explore these questions in full before you get to the theme plates. You're going to be armed with the insights and just all these ideas freshen your mind and nice and intimate with your audience. And then we're going to channel all of that and plug it into a set of high converting theme plates that you could plug into your funnel right away. So I'll catch you in the first theme plate.
